_doc: Append-only ledger of versioned migration artifacts for the first-party
core store schema (buildCreateStoreSchemaQueries / createStoreTables in
src/lib/drizzle-store.ts). See migrations/README.md for the convention:
every migrations/core/core__NNNN_short-description.mjs runner module
ships with an entry here, in the same PR as the schema change it
accompanies. Entry shape:
seq         — the NNNN sequence number, as a string ('0001')
file        — the artifact path relative to migrations/ (new entries:
'core/core__NNNN_short-description.mjs'; seq 0001/0002
predate the node-pg-migrate runner and point at the
legacy psql artifacts — their runner forms live at
core/core__0001_… and core/core__0002_…)
summary     — one line: what the migration does and why
destructive — true when the change affects user-land data (the case
that REQUIRES an artifact); false for optional artifacts
shipped with additive changes (e.g. long backfills)
tables      — the affected core-store table names (no schema prefix;
the schema name is variable, see the README)
Never renumber or rewrite an entry once its migration has shipped;
supersede it with a new sequence number.

migrations.summary: Add notifications.dedupe_key + partial unique index (user_id, dedupe_key) for the general per-user notification dedupe (issue #50). Required by the unique-index-on-existing-table rule; cannot collide in practice — the column is new, so every pre-existing row is outside the partial-index predicate.

Drop the dead agent_templates.durable column (issue #84). No routing/execution code ever read it and every writer only persisted the default false, so no user-land data is lost; required by the drop-column-on-existing-table rule.

Drop the dormant extension_migrations ledger (issue #118): it backed the RETIRED extension JSON-DSL migration machinery and was empty on every lineage (no extension ever declared cinatra.migrations). Extension migrations now record into the shared node-pg-migrate pgmigrations ledger under ext_<scope>_<pkg>__NNNN names; required by the drop-table rule.

Demote the 17 anchor rows the requiredExtensions shrink (cinatra#7) removed from the prod bootable set (33 -> 16): required_in_prod -> false and locked -> active for exactly those rows; archived tombstones stay archived; nothing is uninstalled and all provenance/dependency/owner state is preserved. Fresh databases seed from the current declaration and ledger-fake the chain; idempotent on re-run.

Convert the extension install-op journal to APPEND-ONLY (cinatra#158): drop the two single-row-per-(package,org) unique indexes (pkg_org_uniq / pkg_global_uniq), add partial-UNIQUE indexes enforcing at most one finalized op per (package,org) (the install anchor) + the global org_id-IS-NULL twin, add a (package_name, org_id, phase) scan index, and widen the phase CHECK to admit the new terminal 'superseded' phase. Existing rows are preserved (append-only is strictly more permissive than single-row, so the new partial-unique-on-finalized cannot collide: a single-row schema had at most one row per (package,org), hence at most one finalized). COORDINATED, NON-ROLLING boundary: apply with pre-0005 writers drained — old reset-on-begin UPDATE keyed by (package,org) could touch multiple appended rows once the full unique indexes are gone.

One-time, lossy-by-design canonicalization of legacy dashboard configs to the v1.2 analytics envelope (cinatra#327; owner decision eng#206 — NO backward compat, the strict validator stays strict and #329 deletes the legacy parser). Rewrites every dashboards row AND dashboard_revisions row at config_version IN ('1.0.0','1.1.0') into the SAME single-analytics-portlet v1.2 envelope #326's wrapDcAsV12 emits ({apiVersion:'v1.2', scopeLevel:<derived>, portlets:[{instanceId/kind:'analytics', version:'1.0.0', slot:'fixed', config:{dashboard:<normalized 1.1-shaped config>, __cinatraMigration:'core__0006'}}]}) and sets config_version='v1.2'. A JS-side TOTAL normalizer (bundled into the module; pinned EQUIVALENT to the package validators by a unit test) runs INSIDE the migration transaction via parameterized UPDATE ... SET config_json=$1::jsonb: it NEVER throws and ALWAYS yields a config.dashboard passing the analytics kind's strict DashboardConfigV1_1Schema deep validator, repairing not just well-formed 1.0/1.1 bodies but corrupt-at-rest shapes (scalar/null/array root -> minimal-valid-empty dashboard; dashboardFilterMapping:'bad'->[]; eagerLoad:'yes'->dropped; grid:{cols:-1}->dropped; layoutMode:'freeform'->'grid'; colorPalette:42->dropped; non-object portlet element dropped; missing/empty id -> deterministic unique id; missing content-spec -> analysisConfig:{}); conforming known data is preserved byte-equivalent (passthrough keys kept). Each wrapped envelope is FINAL-VALIDATED by the bundled validator before write; any failure ABORTS the transaction. A migration MARKER is stamped at portlets[0].config.__cinatraMigration (a strict-allowed opaque-record slot) so down() reverts ONLY rows THIS migration produced. scopeLevel derives from the row's project_id/template_scope (-> 'project') else owner_level; revisions JOIN dashboards on dashboard_id for scope (revisions carry no scope columns). Both tables are LOCKed IN SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE MODE before the selects (the runner's advisory lock serializes schema work, not app dashboard writes) and a zero-legacy COUNT(*) postcondition asserts no 1.0/1.1 row remains. Also flips the EXISTING-DB dashboards.config_version column DEFAULT '1.0.0' -> 'v1.2' (distinct from the fresh-install bootstrap-DDL/Drizzle-mirror default, also flipped this PR). Idempotent (the legacy-version predicate excludes already-v1.2 rows; the normalizer is a fixed point on conforming bodies). down() reverses ONLY marker-bearing single-analytics-portlet rows (exactly one portlet AND portlets[0].kind='analytics' AND portlets[0].config.__cinatraMigration='core__0006' AND extension_id IS NULL), unwrapping back to the bare DC config + config_version '1.1.0' and resetting the column default, so genuine extension v1.2 rows, NON-migrated multi-portlet operator v1.2 rows (analytics-first + siblings), AND native #326 single-analytics operator v1.2 rows (no marker) are all untouched.

Add the agent_run_pm_links link table for the schedule↔PM-task sync (cinatra#317): one row per schedule-defining trigger mirrored to an external project-management provider (Plane), keyed by run_id (one-to-one with agent_run_triggers) with FK run_id → agent_runs.id ON DELETE CASCADE, plus a (provider) scan index. Columns: provider (PM provider id), external_task_id (provider work-item id; null until first successful push), synced_at (last successful mirror), sync_error (last fail-open error; null=healthy), version (optimistic-concurrency counter for the reconcile loop #318). ADDITIVE (a brand-new table) so no artifact is strictly required — it rides the idempotent bootstrap DDL (buildCreateStoreSchemaQueries adds the same CREATE TABLE/INDEX this PR); the module ships anyway as a pure CREATE … IF NOT EXISTS to keep the fresh-bootstrap and migration paths aligned and give the table a ledgered row (no-op on a bootstrap-seeded schema, ledger-faked on a fresh install). down() drops the index then the table.

Add the webhook_idempotency leased dedupe ledger for the generic inbound-webhook route (cinatra#340): one row per (scope, site_id, message_id) where scope is '<vendor>/<slug>/<hook>' and message_id is the Standard-Webhooks webhook-id. Leased state machine (processing | done | failed) with lease_until + attempt_count (the finalize fence) so a crashed holder's lease expires and a later arrival re-claims, and a stale holder cannot overwrite a newer attempt's verdict. All three key columns NOT NULL with a UNIQUE index (scope, site_id, message_id); site_id uuid (connect_sites.site_id identity space, D1b). ADDITIVE (a brand-new table) — rides the idempotent bootstrap DDL (buildCreateStoreSchemaQueries adds the same CREATE TABLE/INDEX this PR); the module ships anyway as a pure CREATE … IF NOT EXISTS (no-op on a bootstrap-seeded schema, ledger-faked on a fresh install). down() drops the index then the table.

Add the webhook_secret_bindings table for the per-webhook/per-site secret service (cinatra#340): one active row per (vendor, slug, hook, site) holding the Standard-Webhooks secret material ENCRYPTED via the host secretsCodec (current_secret_ciphertext/iv + an optional previous_secret_ciphertext/iv for the bounded dual-secret rotation window, previous_expires_at, rotated_at), resolved by the server-issued opaque binding_id (PRIMARY KEY) — never from the request payload. site_id uuid (connect_sites.site_id space, D1b). A partial UNIQUE index (vendor, slug, hook, site_id) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL enforces at most one active binding per tuple; revoked_at tombstones a binding. legacy_enabled is the structural hook for the #343 legacy single-shared-secret bridge (its legacy-secret STORAGE columns are deferred to #343 per D3c option A; stays false in #340). ADDITIVE (a brand-new table) — rides the idempotent bootstrap DDL; ships as a pure CREATE … IF NOT EXISTS (no-op on a bootstrap-seeded schema, ledger-faked on a fresh install). down() drops the indexes then the table.

Add the webhook_outbound_dead_letter table for the host-owned OUTBOUND webhook delivery engine (cinatra#341): the durable dead-letter record the pre-#341 fire-and-forget assistant-webhook path lacked. The WEBHOOK_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY BullMQ dispatcher arm writes one row when a delivery is classified `permanent` (non-retryable 4xx, missing url/secret, or a non-decodable legacy secret the standardwebhooks signer rejects) OR when all configured `attempts` are exhausted on a `retryable` result. Columns: event_kind (e.g. 'assistant.mention'), message_id (the Standard-Webhooks webhook-id / receiver idempotency key), target_url (reduced to origin+pathname — query string + userinfo stripped so a URL token can't leak), payload_digest (sha256 hex of the serialized payload — NEVER the raw payload or secret), attempts, last_status (int|null), last_error (truncated+scrubbed text), failed_at, created_at. A UNIQUE index (event_kind, message_id) makes the DLQ insert idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — a permanent row and a later last-attempt-retryable row for the same identity collapse to one) and a failed_at scan index backs the operator view. ADDITIVE (a brand-new table) — rides the idempotent bootstrap DDL (buildCreateStoreSchemaQueries adds the same CREATE TABLE/INDEX this PR); the module ships anyway as a pure CREATE … IF NOT EXISTS (no-op on a bootstrap-seeded schema, ledger-faked on a fresh install). down() drops the indexes then the table.

Add the legacy single-shared-secret STORAGE columns (legacy_secret_ciphertext/iv) to webhook_secret_bindings for the #343 legacy webhook bridge (D3c option A) deferred by core__0009. A binding flagged legacy_enabled keeps its in-field sender's bespoke `sha256=<hex>` HMAC (the deployed WordPress plugin) while still routing through the generic /webhook facility; the shared HMAC secret is stored ENCRYPTED via the host secretsCodec (ciphertext+iv blob, NOT a ref) under a field-scoped AAD (webhook-binding.<binding_id>.legacy) so a legacy blob can never decrypt in the current/previous columns. Both columns nullable — a Standard-Webhooks binding (the forward default) leaves them null. ADDITIVE + idempotent (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS) — rides the idempotent bootstrap DDL (buildCreateStoreSchemaQueries adds the same columns); no-op on a bootstrap-seeded schema, ledger-faked on a fresh install. down() drops the two columns.
